summary: Two-story rectangular small house raised on a one-block-high stonebrick platform with a flat dark roof. Exterior walls are horizontal oak planks with vertical log corner posts, mid-level stonebrick/stone-slab trim between floors, regular glass pane windows in rows on both floors, and a surrounding stonebrick slab border forming a short foundation/walkway.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box, two stacked floors with continuous window rows and corner logs
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along long axis (front/back similar); likely symmetric on short axis as well

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: foundation wall / trim and slab border

materials.name: brick
materials.role: dark roof center (appears like nether brick or dark brick)

elements: rows of glass pane windows on both floors (aligned horizontally)
vertical log corner posts at each exterior corner
mid-level stone trim between first and second floor
stone slab/stonebrick perimeter platform one block high
flat dark brick roof inset within a stone slab rim
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular footprint ~9x5 blocks on ground level; raise a one-block-high stonebrick perimeter (foundation) around it.
Build two 3-block-high stories of horizontal oak planks (or similar wood) with a 1-block high stonebrick band between floors.
Place vertical logs at each corner spanning both floors; inset rows of glass panes (about 3-4 panes per side) centered on each floor wall.
Top with a 1-block-high dark brick (nether-brick-like) roof fill, surrounded by a single layer of stone slabs as a rim.
uncertainties: Exact footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from perspective; actual could differ by +/- 1-2 blocks.
Roof material appears dark (nether brick) but could be a different dark brick variant.
Foundation uses stonebrick and slabs but some visible trim may be slabs vs full blocks; exact slab orientation uncertain.
Interior layout and presence/location of entrance/door are not visible from provided views.
